channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-224] Move fail_loc handling from lustre to libcfs.
item.description: This patch goal is to move the fail_loc handling from lustre to libcfs. The reason being is that currently you can only do fault testing in the lustre core code. By moving this code to libcfs all sub systems will be able to inject faults into the system for testing purposes. This is in particular needed to support the cray gemini klnd driver since one can inject faults for testing failure conditions.
item.environment: Lustre 2.1 version on both cray and non cray environments
item.summary: Move fail_loc handling from lustre to libcfs.

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.

comments.comment: Landed for 2.1
This ticket can be closed now.

Integrated in lustre-master » i686,server,el6,inkernel #129
LU-224 Move fail_loc handling from lustre to libcfs

Oleg Drokin : a7f5b2e19d5645c3a54e957bdc20fa10cacf0724
Files :

  • lustre/obdclass/linux/linux-sysctl.c
  • lustre/tests/sanity-gss.sh
  • lustre/ost/ost_handler.c

  • lustre/obdclass/class_obd.c
  • lustre/tests/conf-sanity.sh
  • libcfs/include/libcfs/libcfs.h
  • libcfs/libcfs/fail.c
  • lustre/llite/file.c
  • libcfs/libcfs/darwin/darwin-proc.c
  • lustre/liblustre/tests/recovery_small.c
  • lustre/mdt/mdt_handler.c
  • lustre/ptlrpc/client.c
  • libcfs/libcfs/linux/linux-proc.c
  • libcfs/include/libcfs/Makefile.am
  • libcfs/libcfs/Makefile.in
  • lustre/include/obd_support.h
  • lustre/tests/replay-single.sh
  • lustre/obdclass/darwin/darwin-sysctl.c
  • libcfs/include/libcfs/libcfs_fail.h
  • lustre/lov/lov_request.c
  • lustre/lvfs/lvfs_lib.c
  • lustre/mdt/mdt_internal.h
  • lustre/ptlrpc/service.c
  • libcfs/libcfs/module.c
  • lustre/include/darwin/obd_support.h
  • lustre/mgs/mgs_handler.c
  • lustre/obdfilter/filter.c
  • lustre/ldlm/ldlm_request.c

Oops. Now that jekins works it appears this patch breaks liblustre which I don't build by default. Testings and I will push a new patch soon.
Just did. Also I updated the License. I asked at a community call about how to License new code. I was told not to go under OpenSFS but what organization you work for. So I updated that.
James, if the build env is fixed, should you post a new patch to kick off Jenkins again?
Yes. I believe whamcloud has a new build environment.
Has the problem with the build been identified yet?
Thanks. For some reason the build goes into the queue but never gets out. Its not a build error in the normal sense.
Looks like the build is still failing though.

Hi, James. Change #450 looks good to me. I'd give it a +1 in Gerrit, but I can't add myself as a reviewer either (I screwed up my account by using two different OpenIDs). I don't need to re-review, but were you going to add an OpenSFS Copyright to libcfs_fail.h? I'm not sure what the policy should be at this point.

Cory I have another patch with the fixes you wanted. http://review.whamcloud.com/#change,450. For some reason I could not push it with the orignal change id. Also i couldn't add you as a reviewer. Can you add yourself?
The hudson build failed due to a queue back log. Is there a way to request a restart to the Hudson build
Assigning to Oleg - he's probably the best person to review this, or to decide who else should do it.
Who should inspect the patch form whamcloud?
